title: gaylussacia rugosa
intro: Gaylussacia rugosa, also known as the hairy chokeberry or downy haw, is a species of shrub native to eastern North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gaylussacia rugosa include its ability to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and act as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Gaylussacia rugosa may be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
